sutta: the buddha said monks if any monk abuses and reviles rails at the aryans who are who are his fellows in the holy life it is utterly impossible it is unavoidable that he should not come to 1 or other of 11 disasters what 11 he fails to attain the unattained 2 from 1 he has attained he falls away 3 true dhamma is not made clear for him 4 he is conceited about true dharmas 5 he follows the holy life without delight 6 he commits some foul offense 7 he gives up the training and falls back to the low life that means the life as a layman 8 he falls into some grievous sickness 9 he goes out of his mind with distraction then he makes an end with mind confused that means he dies with mind confused 11 when body breaks up beyond death he rises up again in the ways the ill-born the downfall in hell monks if any man abuses and reviles rails at the aryans who are his fellows in the holy life it is utterly impossible it is unavoidable that he should not come to 1 or other of these 11 disasters so here that's the end of the sutra
commentary: so here the buddha is saying if we abuse a holy man and aryan here is talking about monks like if monks abuse other holy monks then they can suffer 1 of these 11 um disasters number 1 he fails to attain the unattained that means what uh holy stage yeah aryan stage he has not attained you will feel to get it now number 2 what he ascertained he falls away for example uh if there's attain concentration and then he gets angry with some some holy monks then he might lose his concentration just like devadatta devadatta at 1 time he attained all the 8 janas so that he had psychic power but later because he wanted to take over the buddha's place as leader of the monks and the buddha did not allow him so he schemed to kill the buddha and he talked bad about the buddha so all the psychic power that he had attained all fell away he lost all his psychic power number 3 true dhamma is not made clear for him he is not able to see the true dharma or he is conceited about true dharmas he is swollen hated because he knows a bit of dhamma number 5 he does not delight in the holy life that means he wears the rope he's not happy on number 6 he commits some foul offense he breaks some some precepts some heavy precepts on number 7 he gives up the training as a monk and he goes back to the lay line number 8 he becomes sick grievously sick just like devadatta after trying to kill the buddha later he became grievously ill and then he felt so felt sorry for what he did and then when he wanted to come and apologize to the buddha it was too late it's too late he died before he could see the buddha then number 9 he might go out of his mind with distraction that means his mind is very restless very scattered because of talking bad about holy man so he's very uh his mind is very scattered number 10 him he dies with mind confused when he's about to die his mind is very confused then number 11 after his dinah he might be reborn in hell because of reviling holy men [Music] now suta

items: falls away from what has been attained
true dhamma is not made clear for him
does not delight in the holy life
gives up the training and falls back to the lay life
goes out of his mind with distraction

quiz.quote: If a monk abuses and reviles noble ones, it is unavoidable that he comes to one of eleven disasters.

options.title: Respect noble ones
options.body: Reviling noble companions damages one's own practice and can lead to grave downfall.

options.title: Mere politeness
options.body: The teaching is only about keeping pleasant social manners in the monastery.

options.title: Meditation method
options.body: The passage mainly explains a technique for entering concentration.

options.title: Fixed destiny
options.body: Once a monk makes a mistake, the eleven disasters must all happen to him.

quiz.teacherSummary: The teacher says abusing holy monks can obstruct the path: one may fail to attain, lose concentration or psychic power, become conceited, lose delight in the holy life, break training, become ill or confused, and even be reborn in hell. The point is that reviling noble ones is spiritually dangerous, not merely impolite.

sutta: the buddha said monks 11 advantages are to be looked for from the radiation by mind of loving-kindness by making a loving kindness grow by making much of it by making loving kindness a vehicle and basis by persisting in it by becoming familiar with it by well establishing it what are the 11 1 sleeps happy and wakes happy he dreams no evil dream he is dear to human beings and non-human beings are like the devas or heavenly beings guard him fire poison or sword affect him not quickly he concentrates his mind his complexion is serene he makes an end or he dies without bewilderment it means unconfused and if he has penetrated no further he reaches upon dying the brahma world these 11 advantages are to be looked for from the radiation by mind of loving kindness by making loving-kindness grow by making much of it by making loving-kindness a vehicle and basis by persisting in it by becoming familiar with it by well establishing it that's the end of the suta
commentary: [Music] here i have translated this radiation by mind of loving kindness here this in the polytech society book they have it as the release of heart by the practice of enmity release i uh it's like liberation liberation here i prefer the word radiation are you radiate loving kindness by the mind in the sutas the buddha has said now that to practice loving kindness we should first attain the jana at least the first jana because if a person has attained jhana the mind is strong then when he practices radiating out the loving kindness it is possible but if we don't have a strong mind we try to radiate the loving kindness other beings cannot feel it so if a person can practice this radiation by mind of loving kindness it means he has attained the jhanas and if a person has attained the jhanas so he gets these benefits by practicing methala he sleeps happy and wakes happy he dreams no evil dream he is dear to human beings and non-human beings alike because he's always having meta the devas guard him fire poison or sword affect him not this 1 is because the devas guard him sometimes people think even if he takes poison he won't die but it's not he i don't think it's in that sense la there is a sense that because he is protected by devasa then even if he were to maybe take some some poison or anything or or fire or sword anything might want to harm him but the devas will protect him and then he can concentrate his mind quickly his complexion is very serene and he dies unconfused and he has if he has not attained to arahan hudna he will attain to the brahma world brahma world is in the form [Music] realm rupa loka and in the form realm generally you should have jhana to be able to be reborn then so these are the advantages of practicing meta loving-kindness so with that now i come to the end of the angutra nikaya
